topic: Someone finds an unsigned birthday card in their mailbox
raw_output:
[story 1]

The mail slot rattled with a familiar, unwelcome thud. I pulled the thick envelope out, expecting junk mail or perhaps a bill. Instead, there it was: a card, crisp and cream-colored, completely blank save for a single, elegant script address. My jaw tightened almost imperceptibly. Who on earth thought they could just slip something like this into my delivery? I unfolded it with a deliberate slowness, my thumb tracing the untouched surface. A flicker of sharp annoyance crossed my face. It was utterly presumptuous. I crumpled the card slightly, the sound a small, frustrated snap in the quiet apartment. I tossed it toward the recycling bin, needing to dispose of the audacity immediately.

[story 2]

It was Tuesday, and I was wrestling with the sheer banality of the day when I noticed it tucked beneath the utility statement. An unsigned birthday card. My eyes narrowed as I picked it up, the paper feeling strangely flimsy in my hand. I reread the faded, looping handwriting on the front—a name I didn't recognize. A hot flush rose up my neck. This felt like an invasion of privacy, a casual declaration made without any preamble or context. I stood up abruptly, pacing the small living room. I needed to know who this was and why they felt entitled to send me something so personal without even signing their name. I slammed the mail onto the counter, the resulting sound echoing my rising irritation.

[story 3]

I retrieved the envelope from the slot, my movements stiff with disapproval. Inside, the card lay pristine, a white expanse mocking my expectations. A tight knot formed in my stomach. This wasn't just misplaced mail; this was a deliberate gesture, a silent assertion of some unwarranted familiarity. I stared at the empty space where a signature should have been, my lips pressing into a thin, hard line. I shook my head, a quick, sharp motion. How dare someone assume a relationship, a connection, just based on proximity? I snatched the card and walked straight to the front door, intending to discard the entire package without even looking at it again.
